title: Knowledge base / FAQ
description: Data contract for the knowledge-base format: a navigable help centre / FAQ rendered from a single embedded JSON instance. A sticky category sidebar + a prominent live search box drive a Q&A accordion of discrete question->answer entries grouped by category, each with tags and related-entry links. Not a narrative article (that is long-form / magazine) and not a record grid (that is data-register). The sample's #report-data MUST be a valid instance of this schema.

meta.description: Shared chrome metadata common to every report format (wordmark, title, default theme).

org.description: Owning organisation; brand-neutral placeholder {{ORG}} unless a real value is supplied.

project.description: Project / product the knowledge base belongs to; placeholder {{PROJECT}} unless supplied.

reportType.description: Human label for the artefact kind, e.g. 'Help Centre' or 'Knowledge Base'.

title.description: Knowledge-base title shown in the masthead.

subtitle.description: Optional one-line subtitle / scope under the title.

author.description: Maintainer / owning team; placeholder {{AUTHOR}} unless supplied.

theme.description: Initial brand theme applied via <html data-theme>. 'black' is always selectable in the switcher.

intro.description: Optional short line shown under the masthead title (e.g. an invitation to search).

searchPlaceholder.description: Optional placeholder text for the search box. Defaults to a generic 'Search the knowledge base…' if omitted.

popularTags.description: Optional list of tag strings surfaced as quick-filter chips in the sidebar tags panel. Should be a subset of tags actually used on articles.

categories.description: Sidebar categories used to group and filter the Q&A entries. Order defines sidebar + heading order.

id.description: Stable category identifier; referenced by article.category.

icon.description: Optional house-icon category/name (e.g. 'document', 'status', 'code', 'nav') used as the category mark.

description.description: Optional short description of the category.

articles.description: The Q&A entries. Each is one collapsed accordion card: question (summary) + answer blocks (body) + tags + related links.

id.description: Stable, URL-safe entry id; used for the deep-link hash and as a related[] target.

category.description: Id of the category this entry belongs to (must match a categories[].id).

question.description: The question, shown as the accordion summary. Plain text (rendered escaped).

answerBlocks.description: Ordered answer content blocks. Concatenated text across blocks is what live search matches against.

type.description: Block kind: 'p' prose paragraph, 'code' verbatim mono block, 'list' bullet/numbered list, 'note' tinted callout.

text.description: For 'p' and 'note': the body text. Limited inline HTML allowed (<code>, <strong>, <em>, <a>).

code.description: For 'code': the verbatim code/command shown in a mono dark panel (rendered escaped).

lang.description: For 'code': optional language label shown in the panel header.

caption.description: For 'code': optional caption shown above the panel.

items.description: For 'list': the list items (inline HTML allowed).

ordered.description: For 'list': render as an ordered (numbered) list instead of bulleted.

variant.description: For 'note': callout colour — maps to the foundation states (ok/risk/hold) or a neutral 'info'.

label.description: For 'note': optional bold lead label for the callout (e.g. 'Heads up').

tags.description: Optional tag chips for the entry; clicking a tag activates a tag filter.

related.description: Optional ids of related articles, rendered as 'Related' jump links.

meta.title: Platform Knowledge Base
meta.subtitle: Answers for the ingestion platform — accounts, data, API, and incidents
meta.author: {{AUTHOR}}

examples.intro: Search a question or browse by category. Every answer is self-contained.

categories.label: Getting started
categories.description: First steps: accounts, access, and the basics.

articles.question: How do I create an account and invite my team?

answerBlocks.text: Sign up from the console landing page with a work email. The first user becomes the workspace owner and can invite others from Settings → Members.

items: Enter one or more email addresses.

answerBlocks.label: Tip
answerBlocks.text: Invites expire after 7 days; re-send from the same screen if one lapses.
Roles are workspace-wide and control both console access and API-key scope.

items: Owner — full access, including billing and member management.
Editor — create and edit resources; no billing.
Viewer — read-only access to dashboards and logs.

articles.question: How do I enable single sign-on (SSO)?

answerBlocks.text: SSO uses SAML 2.0 and is available on the Team and Enterprise plans. Configure it under Settings → Security → SSO.

answerBlocks.caption: Example ACS endpoint to register with your IdP

answerBlocks.label: Before you switch
answerBlocks.text: Once SSO is enforced, password logins are disabled for the domain. Keep one break-glass owner with a recovery code.

articles.question: How do I authenticate API requests?

answerBlocks.text: Pass your API key as a bearer token on every request. Create and rotate keys under Settings → API keys.

answerBlocks.label: Never expose keys
answerBlocks.text: Treat keys like passwords — keep them server-side and out of client bundles and git history.

articles.question: How do I rotate an API key without downtime?

items: Create a second key alongside the active one.
Deploy the new key to all services.
Verify traffic shifts to the new key in the usage view.

answerBlocks.label: Zero-downtime
answerBlocks.text: Both keys are valid during the overlap window, so there is no gap in authentication.

articles.question: What are the rate limits and how do I handle 429s?

answerBlocks.text: The default limit is 600 requests/minute per key. Exceeding it returns HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header.
Back off for the indicated seconds and retry with jitter. See the retry guidance for safe patterns.

articles.question: How do webhooks retry on failure?

answerBlocks.text: If your endpoint does not return 2xx within 5 seconds, the delivery is retried with exponential backoff for up to 24 hours.

items: Each attempt carries the same event_id — make handlers idempotent.

articles.question: Where do I find invoices and receipts?

answerBlocks.text: Invoices are issued monthly and available under Settings → Billing → Invoices. Owners can add a billing email to receive them automatically.

articles.question: How do I upgrade or downgrade my plan?

answerBlocks.text: Change plans anytime from Settings → Billing → Plan. Upgrades take effect immediately; downgrades apply at the next renewal.

answerBlocks.label: Proration
answerBlocks.text: Upgrades are prorated for the remainder of the current cycle.

articles.question: How is usage metered and billed?

answerBlocks.text: Usage is metered per processed event and aggregated daily. Your current period's usage is shown live under Billing → Usage.

items: Overage is billed at the per-event rate on your plan.
Set a usage alert to be notified before overage.

articles.question: I'm locked out / lost access to my account — what now?

answerBlocks.text: Use the Recover access link on the login page. If SSO is enforced and your IdP is down, sign in with a break-glass owner recovery code.

answerBlocks.label: No recovery code?
answerBlocks.text: Contact support from a verified domain email; identity verification can take up to one business day.

articles.question: What's the recommended retry strategy for transient errors?

answerBlocks.text: Retry only on 429 and 5xx. Use capped exponential backoff with full jitter, and stop after a bounded number of attempts.

answerBlocks.caption: Backoff with jitter
answerBlocks.code:
delay = min(cap, base * 2 ** attempt)
sleep(random(0, delay))

answerBlocks.label: Don't retry blind
answerBlocks.text: Never retry non-idempotent writes without an idempotency key — you risk duplicating the operation.
